goal: Prevent AI agents from starting from zero every session by establishing a post-project retro loop that captures failures, constraints, and learnings into the database for future agents to retrieve.
when_to_use: After a major failure or unexpected issue
Weekly team retro for cross-project patterns
When adding new rules, checklists, or templates
inputs_required: Access to project logs, error traces, review gate results
steps: Collect: gather what happened during the project (successes, failures, surprises)
Analyze: identify patterns — what worked, what didn't, what constraints emerged
Distill: decide what knowledge belongs in rules vs checklists vs patterns vs templates
Write: update the appropriate database files with new learnings
Index: rebuild processed chunks so retrieval finds the new knowledge
Verify: confirm next agent session can retrieve the learnings
api_calls: POST /brief or search scripts to verify knowledge retrieval
prompt_contract: Retro is mandatory after every Ship — not optional
Learnings must be written to database, not just thought about
Failure patterns are more valuable than success patterns for future agents
failure_modes: Skipping retro because project is done
Writing retro but not rebuilding indexes (knowledge invisible)
Writing retro in a format agents cannot retrieve
handoff_report: List of learnings added to database

quality.notes: Pattern extracted from gstack's retro+learn mechanism. Evidence level is documented rather than tested because retro effectiveness can only be validated over multiple projects.

retrieval.ai_summary: Post-project retrospective learning loop for AI agents. After every Ship, the agent collects successes and failures, distills them into rules/checklists/patterns/templates, writes to the database, rebuilds indexes, and verifies future agents can retrieve the learnings. Prevents agents from starting from zero every session.
